DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, JUL-29-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANTS 180 SANSOME STREET TENANT LLC AND WEWORK COMPANIES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO AND COMPLIANCE WITH 180 SANSOME STREET TENANT LLCS REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF [SET ONE]. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE MODIFICATION OF ADDING A DEADLINE, AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT, AS PLAINTIFF HAS SERVED FURTHER RESPONSES IN WHICH IT HAS AGREED TO PRODUCE NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO EACH OF THE REQUESTS AT ISSUE AS NARROWED BY THE PARTIES IN THEIR NEGOTIATIONS. BOTH PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS WOULD BE UNJUST. PLAINTIFF WILL ENDEAVOR TO COMPLETE ITS PRODUCTION WITH RESPECT TO DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO THE REQUESTS AT ISSUE IN THIS MOTION NO LATER THAN AUGUST 28, 2020. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TONY SCHOENBERG; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR NO. 5229, TEL: 510-887-6889, EMAIL: P2GMOTO@EARTHLINK.NET (302/JPT)

William A. Logan, Jr. (415-344-7110) for Plaintiff/Moving party 300 PROSPECT PROPERTIES, INC.
David A. Perez (206-359-3735) for Defendant/Opposing party 180 Sansome St.
Peggy Tsujimoto (415-828-1919), CSR #5229, p2gmoto@earthlink.net.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To And Compliance With 180 Sansome Street Tenant Llcs Requests For Production Of Documents To Plaintiff [Set One]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCe:
Atty. WILLIAM A. LOGAN, JR., Esq. of Logan Mooney LLP (Tel: 415-738-0764) for Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant 300 Prospect Properties, Inc.;
Atty. ALISHA C. BURGIN, Esq. of Perkins Coie LLP (Tel: 310-788-9900) for Defendants and Cross-Complainant 180 Sansome Street Tenant LLC and WeWork Companies LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS 180 SANSOME STREET TENANT LLC AND WEWORK COMPANIES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To And Compliance With 180 Sansome Street Tenant Llcs Requests For Production Of Documents To Plaintiff [Set One].

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The parties and the judge pro tem all appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

The matter is argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification of adding a deadline, as follows: The motion is denied as moot, as Plaintiff has served further responses in which it has agreed to produce non-privileged documents responsive to each of the requests at issue as narrowed by the parties in their negotiations. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied on the grounds that the imposition of sanctions would be unjust. Plaintiff will endeavor to complete its production with respect to documents in response to the requests at issue in this motion no later than August 28, 2020.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the judge pro tem.

Judge Pro Tem: Tony Schoenberg; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR No. 5229, Tel: 510-887-6889, Email: p2gmoto@earthlink.net (302/JPT)
